title: Overview of Admission 2026

detail.title: Overview of Admission 2026
detail.short_info: Christ (Deemed to be University) Application Form 2026 Session 3 is open from April 1 to May 3, 2026. Apply online now for UG and PG programmes, check eligibility, dates, and step-by-step guide.

content.text: Christ University Application Form 2026 Session 3 UG and PG Programs, such as BBA, BCom, BCA, and BA, is now open from April 1, 2026 to May 3, 2026. Apply online immediately for BBA (Honours/Honours with Research) and other BBA specializations at CHRIST (Deemed to be University).

The university offers through a three-session admission cycle. Session 3 applications are currently open. This article covers the full Christ University important dates 2026, eligibility, application process, fees, entrance test pattern, syllabus, and selection procedure so you can plan your application perfectly.

rows: Entrance Test (ET) + Skill Assessment (SA) / Micro Presentation (MP) / Personal Interview (PI)
Passed Class 12 (10+2) or equivalent from a recognised board
Students appearing for Class 12 in 2026 can apply with Class 11 final report

sections.heading: How to Apply for Christ University Application Form 2026?

items: Register — Visit Christ University Application and create one-time registration using Class 10 name, email & mobile.
Login & Fill Form — Select BBA programme & campus → Upload photo → Enter personal, academic & parent details.
Upload Documents — Class 10 and Class 11/12 marks cards.
Preview & Submit — Generate 9-digit application number. No changes allowed after submission.
Print Application — Save PDF for future reference.

content.text: Direct Apply Link for Christ University BBA 2026 Session 3: Apply Online Now
The BBA Entrance Test (ET) is online, 90 minutes duration with 120 MCQs. Typical sections for BBA:
Negative marking of 0.25 applies. Admit card released 2–3 days before the test.

sections.heading: Documents Required for Christ University Admission 2026

items: Recent passport-size photograph (3.5 cm × 4.5 cm, white background)
Scanned Class 11 final report or Class 12 board result/hall ticket
